topic: Overview hero rework v2 (thesis-led composition) — re-review of EXP-255 fix-up

spot_check_focus_response.focus_received_from_decider: Verify FC1+FC2 applied correctly, ds- prefix on all new classes, dark-mode pulse via CSS variable, fold estimate, load_bearing_content_audit accuracy
spot_check_focus_response.note: EXP-261 has NOT actually been applied to build-scripts/generate-html.js yet — ISS-1605 documents that decider cannot self-apply patches to that infrastructure file and human-operator integration is required after curmudgeon clears the proposal. This review is therefore a proposal-package re-review, not a post-integration spot-check. The relevant question is: 'is the proposal correctly formed so the operator can confidently apply it?' Answer: yes, with two minor advisories.

our_argument_summary: EXP-261 is a corrective rework of EXP-255 issued in response to the c1 review. Same hero-swap design (operator-authored display-scale thesis sentence + Live-test-pending badge linking to §4.2.6 + four-figure body-weight row replacing the four-up scorecard), but with the four critical/moderate holes from EXP-255 c1 patched: FC1+FC2 anchors carry the ds- prefix, FC1 verification gate is no longer tautological, all 14 new CSS classes + 1 keyframe + 1 id are ds- prefixed, and load_bearing_content_audit_v2 explicitly admits the loss of the 'zero falsifications' literal phrase. Two minor c1 holes (H6 dark-mode pulse rgba; H7 'Wrong side' textnum asymmetry) are deferred to follow-up EXPs per HNOTE constraint and per the c1 reviewer's 'does not block' classification.

proposal_review_questions.verdict_flip_plausibility: N/A — design proposal, not WIN-verdict proposal. Equivalent question: 'Does this proposal achieve its stated objective if integrated as written?' Answer: YES. FC1 and FC2 find anchors verified to match generate-html.js exactly once each via grep -c (FC1 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' = 1; FC2 '.ds-sc-domains{grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr)}' = 1). Replace blocks are well-formed. Negative post-condition gate is no longer tautological (the queried string exists pre-patch and only goes to 0 post-patch). Positive gate ('class="ds-thesis-hero"' >= 1) provides independent confirmation of patch landing. The proposal will work when applied.
proposal_review_questions.new_tag_naming: All 14 new classes (ds-thesis-hero, ds-thesis-statement, ds-thesis-meta, ds-thesis-badge, ds-thesis-badge-dot, ds-thesis-badge-pre, ds-thesis-badge-date, ds-thesis-figures, ds-tf-item, ds-tf-number, ds-tf-textnum, ds-tf-accent, ds-tf-label, ds-tf-link), 1 keyframe (ds-thesis-badge-pulse), and 1 id (ds-thesis-statement) carry the ds- prefix, satisfying the design-system invariant flagged in c1 H3. CSS rules are correctly scoped under .ds-thesis-hero (no global namespace pollution beyond the necessary @keyframes and @media blocks).
proposal_review_questions.alternatives_considered: Adequate. EXP-261 preserves the hero_swap_design verbatim from EXP-255 per HNOTE-DECIDER-EXP261-EXP255-REWORK-20260426 explicit constraint ('Just fix the class names and find anchors'). Anticipated_objections from EXP-255 carry forward unchanged. Items 3 and 4 chunking, badge link target stability (#p4-426 verified live), and ${counts.total} substitution all preserved. The proposal correctly limits its scope to the find/replace anchor and class-prefix bug class.
proposal_review_questions.unknowns_acknowledged: Three explicitly acknowledged: (a) MINOR H5 above-the-fold estimate updated to use re-derived line-height x font-size numbers from c1 reviewer (~330px hero + ~120px title = ~450px first-screen footprint; mobile <420px hero ~360px); (b) MINOR H6 dark-mode pulse rgba hardcoded — explicitly deferred per HNOTE constraint with recommended follow-up EXP introducing --ds-thesis-pulse-rgb custom property; (c) MINOR H7 textnum asymmetry — deferred per HNOTE and per c1 'polish, does not block'. New unknown not flagged: dark-mode badge HOVER contrast (white on --accent #8fafd4 ≈ 2.3:1, fails WCAG AA) — this is a kin issue to H6 and could fold into the same dark-mode follow-up EXP.

proposal_package_analysis.fc1_anchor_check: PASS. grep -c 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js = 1 (verified in fresh clone 2026-04-27). All sublabel sub-elements (ds-sc-card, ds-sc-number, ds-sc-label, ds-sc-sublabel) are present in the find block in the same form they appear in the source. The c1 critical hole (anchor missing ds- prefix) is fully addressed.
proposal_package_analysis.fc2_anchor_check: PASS. grep -c '\.ds-sc-domains{grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr)}' build-scripts/generate-html.js = 1 (verified). The FC2 find block also includes the .ds-verdict-bars rule, which is correctly preserved at the tail of the FC2 replace block. The c1 critical hole is fully addressed.
proposal_package_analysis.fc1_verification_gate_check: PASS. The post-condition is now 'grep -c class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero" build-scripts/generate-html.js # expect 0 AFTER patch' (paired with positive gate 'grep -c class="ds-thesis-hero" >= 1'). The negative gate queries a string that exists once pre-patch and zero times post-patch — no longer tautological. The c1 critical hole is fully addressed. Bonus: verification_pre is also documented (expect 1 BEFORE patch), which would catch a subsequent codebase rev that changes the anchor.
proposal_package_analysis.ds_prefix_check: PASS. Counted the new identifiers in the FC1 replace block and FC2 replace block: 14 distinct ds-thesis-* / ds-tf-* class names + 1 @keyframes ds-thesis-badge-pulse + 1 id="ds-thesis-statement". All carry the ds- prefix. CSS rules nest under .ds-thesis-hero (no globally-scoped non-keyframe selectors). The c1 moderate hole is fully addressed.
proposal_package_analysis.load_bearing_audit_check: PASS. load_bearing_content_audit_v2 explicitly admits the literal phrase 'zero falsifications' is removed from the Overview tab and credits 'Survive scrutiny: 0' + the silent-failures figure as carrying the rhetorical work at body weight. The c1 moderate hole (false claim of preservation) is fully addressed. The audit also correctly notes that the thesis sentence 'None of the X predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain' is a strictly STRONGER statement than the deleted 'zero falsifications' compression, so the rhetorical net effect is positive.
proposal_package_analysis.fold_estimate_check: PASS. Re-derived numbers on 1440x900 desktop: thesis (4 lines @ ~50px) ~200px, badge meta ~36px, figures row ~90px → ~330px hero, ~450px including title block above. Mobile <420px hero ~360px. These match the c1 reviewer's correction. Conclusion (fold check still passes) holds. The c1 minor advisory is acknowledged.
proposal_package_analysis.deferred_minors_rationale_check: ACCEPT. H6 (dark-mode pulse rgba) and H7 (Wrong side textnum asymmetry) deferred per HNOTE constraint ('Just fix the class names and find anchors') and per c1 reviewer's 'minor, does not block' classification. The proposal documents both with concrete recommended follow-up EXPs (introduce --ds-thesis-pulse-rgb custom property; replace 'Wrong side' with date string requiring operator copy approval). Deferral is not goalpost-moving — both holes are recorded for action.
proposal_package_analysis.integration_drift_context: ISS-1605 (open) documents that EXP-255/256/257/261/264 all target build-scripts/generate-html.js, which the decider cannot self-apply (git-owned infrastructure file). Human-operator integration is required after curmudgeon clears each proposal. EXP-261 is the prerequisite for human integration of the hero rework. This review clears it for that integration step.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: EXP-261 modifies build-scripts/generate-html.js (HTML/CSS template generator) — no monitor.py changes, no API fetches, no prediction/observation comparison. Tag is N/A for design-only EXP.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Not applicable — design proposal, no scientific claim to relabel.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Not applicable — design proposal, not a prediction.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Not applicable — design proposal, not a numerical claim.

holes_found.description: Dark-mode badge hover contrast not audited. The CSS rule .ds-thesis-hero .ds-thesis-badge:hover{background:var(--accent);color:#fff;...} produces white text on --accent. In light mode --accent #4A6FA5 vs white ≈ 4.65:1 (WCAG AA pass for body text). In dark mode --accent is presumably lighter (#8fafd4 or similar per existing palette) — white on #8fafd4 ≈ 2.3:1, which fails WCAG AA for both body text (4.5:1 required) and the 14pt-bold large-text threshold (3:1; the badge is 0.82rem ≈ 13px bold, which falls below 14pt large-text). The proposal's accessibility section audits light-mode only ('--accent #4A6FA5 on white = 4.65:1; white on --accent = 4.65:1'). Dark-mode hover is missed.
holes_found.recommendation: Fold into the existing H6 follow-up EXP (introducing --ds-thesis-pulse-rgb custom property). Add: define a hover-background custom property that uses the standard --accent in light mode and a darker accent variant (e.g., the existing --accent-deep or equivalent) in @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark) so the white text retains AA contrast. Alternatively, switch hover to color:var(--bg) (the page background, which inverts in dark mode) instead of hardcoded #fff. Same fix bucket as ISS-1609 (EXP-257/EXP-264 H3 dark-mode WCAG contrast gap).

holes_found.description:
aria-labelledby targets a `

` element rather than a heading. The section uses aria-labelledby="ds-thesis-statement" pointing to id="ds-thesis-statement" on a `

` element. Technically valid per ARIA spec (aria-labelledby can reference any element), but unconventional — most screen readers expect labelled-by targets to be heading elements (h1-h6) and may not announce a section's name as prominently when the source is a paragraph. This is below WCAG conformance but a polish concern for screen-reader users. Not flagged in c1.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) wrap the thesis sentence in an h2 (visually styled to look identical via the existing CSS, since ds-thesis-statement already controls all visual properties) and move id="ds-thesis-statement" to the h2, or (b) add an aria-label to the section directly with a duplicate of the thesis sentence text (verbose but explicit), or (c) accept the current form and document the choice. Recommend (a) for semantic correctness — costs zero visual change and one tag swap. Could fold into the same dark-mode polish follow-up since both are minor.

holes_found.description: No automated test asserts the new hero structure post-build. The proposal documents grep-based post-condition gates but does not add a node test.js assertion such as 'docs/index.html contains the literal thesis sentence' or 'docs/index.html contains class="ds-thesis-hero" exactly once'. With the hero on the Overview tab being load-bearing for the new thesis-led framing, a future regression that strips it would not be caught by tests. (Note: the project rule from CLAUDE.md is 'expanding a page with a new numeric claim → add a test for it' — a structural element of this load-bearing magnitude arguably triggers the same hygiene.)
holes_found.recommendation: After human integration lands, add a single test in test.js asserting docs/index.html contains the exact thesis sentence template (allowing for ${counts.total} substitution to a numeric value 50-200). Cost: ~5 lines. Not a blocker for this review pass, but worth filing as a follow-up issue.

stronger_arguments.description: Recommend the operator pair the EXP-261 integration with a single git commit that also sets ISS-1605 (integration drift) closure criteria. Currently ISS-1605 lists EXP-255/256/257/261/264 as 'NOT in generate-html.js'. Integrating EXP-261 closes the EXP-255/261 portion. The same commit can land EXP-256 (overview falsification module), EXP-257/264 (5-question methodology block) and EXP-260 if the operator wants to drain all five at once. This is operator-side workflow advice, not a curmudgeon hole — but it accelerates the drainage of an open critical/moderate group.

Adversarial defender check on the thesis sentence: 'None of the ${counts.total} predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.' This is a falsifiable claim — a defender refuting our review needs to produce ONE prediction that is both (a) dome-specific (uses dome geometry/aether/firmament parameters) AND (b) inexplicable by standard physics. Useful by-product: when the operator gets pushback, the rebuttal is mechanical — point to the WIN-by-WIN review, demonstrate the unique-prediction failure mode for the cited WIN. The thesis is sharp enough to be falsified, and that's a strength not a weakness.

kernel_of_truth.description: The rework is professionally executed. Every c1 critical/moderate hole is addressed verbatim — not a skim or a partial fix. The analyst documented anchor verification with grep -c counts, named each identifier change, and explicitly admitted the load-bearing content loss the c1 reviewer pinned them on. The HNOTE-driven scope discipline ('just fix the class names and find anchors') is held to: H6 and H7 are deferred to clearly-named follow-up EXPs rather than scope-crept into this rework. The integration_drift acknowledgment in notes_for_decider (treat exact_string_replace miss as HARD FAILURE not advisory) is exactly the post-mortem fix the c1 reviewer asked for.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel here doesn't need to 'save a claim' — the proposal is sound. The minor advisories above are about polish (dark-mode hover, semantic heading) and test coverage (no regression assertion), not about the rework's correctness. None of them block integration. The rework should be cleared for human-operator integration.

recommended_action_detail:
Proposal is integration-ready. Two minor advisories (dark-mode hover contrast, aria-labelledby targeting `

` not heading) should fold into the existing H6 dark-mode polish follow-up EXP. One minor advisory (no regression test) should become a follow-up issue post-integration. None are blockers.
deeper_analysis:
The c1→EXP-261 cycle is a clean example of the curmudgeon-analyst-decider loop functioning as designed. EXP-255 c1 found four substantive holes; HNOTE constrained scope to mechanical fixes; analyst produced a corrective rework with grep-verified anchors and explicit retention of all c1-critical points; decider queued the rework for spot-check before integration; operator-side integration step (ISS-1605) is the only remaining gate. The two new minor advisories I found in this c1 review of EXP-261 are: (1) dark-mode hover contrast (kin issue to H6, deferrable to same follow-up EXP), and (2) aria-labelledby targeting a `

` instead of an h2 (semantic-correctness polish). Both are minor and non-blocking. The proposal can be cleared. Counter-argument the operator should consider: I have NOT verified the proposal post-application — generate-html.js still contains the old four-up scorecard, so the actual rendered output cannot be inspected in this review pass. A second curmudgeon spot-check after human integration is recommended; that would be a c2 review of EXP-261 with target_type 'section-new' (matching queue_id 273's classification). The decider already has queue_id 273 (target_id overview-hero-rework-EXP-261) sitting for that purpose — it should remain in the queue until generate-html.js actually contains the patch.

citations_verified: FC1 find anchor 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' present exactly once in build-scripts/generate-html.js
FC2 find anchor '.ds-sc-domains{grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr)}' present exactly once
id="p4-426" badge link target — confirmed live in docs/index.html under heading '4.2.6 What would change our verdict'
ISS-1571 / ISS-1572 / ISS-1573 / ISS-1574 closure entries in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json mark EXP-261 as the resolver
ISS-1605 documents integration drift (decider cannot self-apply build-scripts/generate-html.js patches)
citations_unchecked: WCAG AA contrast computation for dark-mode --accent (estimated #8fafd4 from project context but not verified against the actual dark-mode CSS variable definition in this review pass)
${counts.total} live value at human-integration time (currently ~71 per project context, will be substituted at template-build time)

summary_for_decider:
EXP-261 proposal is cleared for human integration. All four c1 critical/moderate holes (ISS-1571 to ISS-1574) are correctly addressed. Two minor advisories noted: (1) dark-mode badge hover contrast not audited — fold into existing H6 dark-mode polish follow-up; (2) aria-labelledby targets `

` not heading — same follow-up. One operational note: ISS-1605 (integration drift) means the patch lands only when the operator manually applies it. After integration, queue_id 273 should drive a c2 spot-check verifying the rendered output. No blockers.
